def solve(nums, queries)
  # Precompute popcount-depth for each number in nums
  # Since k can be up to 5, we can precompute up to depth 5
  # For each number, we compute its popcount-depth
  popcount_depths = nums.map do |num|
    depth = 0
    current = num
    while current > 0
      current = current.bit_count
      depth += 1
      break if current == 1
    end
    depth
  end

  # Build a segment tree for range queries and point updates
  class SegmentTree
    attr_accessor :size, :tree, :lazy

    def initialize(data)
      @size = data.size
      @tree = Array.new(4 * @size, 0)
      @lazy = Array.new(4 * @size, 0)
      build(1, 0, @size - 1, data)
    end

    def build(node, l, r, data)
      if l == r
        @tree[node] = data[l]
        return
      end
      mid = (l + r) / 2
      build(2 * node, l, mid, data)
      build(2 * node + 1, mid + 1, r, data)
      @tree[node] = @tree[2 * node] + @tree[2 * node + 1]
    end

    def update_range(node, l, r, ul, ur, val)
      if ur < l || ul > r
        return
      end
      if ul <= l && r <= ur
        @tree[node] = val
        @lazy[node] = val
        return
      end
      push_down(node, l, r)
      mid = (l + r) / 2
      update_range(2 * node, l, mid, ul, ur, val)
      update_range(2 * node + 1, mid + 1, r, ul, ur, val)
      @tree[node] = @tree[2 * node] + @tree[2 * node + 1]
    end

    def query_range(node, l, r, ql, qr)
      if qr < l || ql > r
        return 0
      end
      if ql <= l && r <= qr
        return @tree[node]
      end
      push_down(node, l, r)
      mid = (l + r) / 2
      left = query_range(2 * node, l, mid, ql, qr)
      right = query_range(2 * node + 1, mid + 1, r, ql, qr)
      left + right
    end

    def push_down(node, l, r)
      if @lazy[node] != 0 && l != r
        mid = (l + r) / 2
        left = 2 * node
        right = 2 * node + 1
        @tree[left] = @lazy[node]
        @lazy[left] = @lazy[node]
        @tree[right] = @lazy[node]
        @lazy[right] = @lazy[node]
        @lazy[node] = 0
      end
    end
  end

  # Create segment tree
  st = SegmentTree.new(popcount_depths)

  # Process queries
  answer = []

  queries.each do |q|
    if q[0] == 1
      l = q[1]
      r = q[2]
      k = q[3]
      count = st.query_range(1, 0, st.size - 1, l, r)
      answer << count
    else
      idx = q[1]
      val = q[2]
      # Update the value in nums and the corresponding popcount-depth
      new_val = val
      new_depth = 0
      current = new_val
      while current > 0
        current = current.bit_count
        new_depth += 1
        break if current == 1
      end
      st.update_range(1, 0, st.size - 1, idx, idx, new_depth)
    end
  end

  answer
end

def solve(nums, queries)
  # Precompute popcount-depth for each number in nums
  # Since k can be up to 5, we can precompute up to depth 5
  # For each number, we compute its popcount-depth
  popcount_depths = nums.map do |num|
    depth = 0
    current = num
    while current > 0
      current = current.bit_count
      depth += 1
      break if current == 1
    end
    depth
  end

  # Build a segment tree for range queries and point updates
  class SegmentTree
    attr_accessor :size, :tree, :lazy

    def initialize(data)
      @size = data.size
      @tree = Array.new(4 * @size, 0)
      @lazy = Array.new(4 * @size, 0)
      build(1, 0, @size - 1, data)
    end

    def build(node, l, r, data)
      if l == r
        @tree[node] = data[l]
        return
      end
      mid = (l + r) / 2
      build(2 * node, l, mid, data)
      build(2 * node + 1, mid + 1, r, data)
      @tree[node] = @tree[2 * node] + @tree[2 * node + 1]
    end

    def update_range(node, l, r, ul, ur, val)
      if ur < l || ul > r
        return
      end
      if ul <= l && r <= ur
        @tree[node] = val
        @lazy[node] = val
        return
      end
      push_down(node, l, r)
      mid = (l + r) / 2
      update_range(2 * node, l, mid, ul, ur, val)
      update_range(2 * node + 1, mid + 1, r, ul, ur, val)
      @tree[node] = @tree[2 * node] + @tree[2 * node + 1]
    end

    def query_range(node, l, r, ql, qr)
      if qr < l || ql > r
        return 0
      end
      if ql <= l && r <= qr
        return @tree[node]
      end
      push_down(node, l, r)
      mid = (l + r) / 2
      left = query_range(2 * node, l, mid, ql, qr)
      right = query_range(2 * node + 1, mid + 1, r, ql, qr)
      left + right
    end

    def push_down(node, l, r)
      if @lazy[node] != 0 && l != r
        mid = (l + r) / 2
        left = 2 * node
        right = 2 * node + 1
        @tree[left] = @lazy[node]
        @lazy[left] = @lazy[node]
        @tree[right] = @lazy[node]
        @lazy[right] = @lazy[node]
        @lazy[node] = 0
      end
    end
  end

  # Create segment tree
  st = SegmentTree.new(popcount_depths)

  # Process queries
  answer = []

  queries.each do |q|
    if q[0] == 1
      l = q[1]
      r = q[2]
      k = q[3]
      count = st.query_range(1, 0, st.size - 1, l, r)
      answer << count
    else
      idx = q[1]
      val = q[2]
      # Update the value in nums and the corresponding popcount-depth
      new_val = val
      new_depth = 0
      current = new_val
      while current > 0
        current = current.bit_count
        new_depth += 1
        break if current == 1
      end
      st.update_range(1, 0, st.size - 1, idx, idx, new_depth)
    end
  end

  answer

      depth += 1
      break if current == 1

      @tree = Array.new(4 * @size, 0)
      @lazy = Array.new(4 * @size, 0)
      build(1, 0, @size - 1, data)
    end

    def build(node, l, r, data)
      if l == r
        @tree[node] = data[l]
        return
      end
      mid = (l + r) / 2
      build(2 * node, l, mid, data)
      build(2 * node + 1, mid + 1, r, data)
      @tree[node] = @tree[2 * node] + @tree[2 * node + 1]
    end

    def update_range(node, l, r, ul, ur, val)
      if ur < l || ul > r
        return
      end
      if ul <= l && r <= ur
        @tree[node] = val
        @lazy[node] = val
        return

      push_down(node, l, r)
      mid = (l + r) / 2
      update_range(2 * node, l, mid, ul, ur, val)
      update_range(2 * node + 1, mid + 1, r, ul, ur, val)
      @tree[node] = @tree[2 * node] + @tree[2 * node + 1]
    end

      left = query_range(2 * node, l, mid, ql, qr)
      right = query_range(2 * node + 1, mid + 1, r, ql, qr)

def solve(nums, queries)
  # Precompute popcount-depth for each number in nums

  # For each number, we compute its popcount-depth
  popcount_depths = nums.map do |num|
    depth = 0
    current = num
    while current > 0
      current = current.bit_count
      depth += 1
      break if current == 1
    end
    depth

  # Build a segment tree for range queries and point updates

    def initialize(data)

      @tree = Array.new(4 * @size, 0)
      @lazy = Array.new(4 * @size, 0)
      build(1, 0, @size - 1, data)
    end

    def build(node, l, r, data)
      if l == r
        @tree[node] = data[l]
        return
      end
      mid = (l + r) / 2
      build(2 * node, l, mid, data)
      build(2 * node + 1, mid + 1, r, data)
      @tree[node] = @tree[2 * node] + @tree[2 * node + 1]
    end

    def update_range(node, l, r, ul, ur, val)
      if ur < l || ul > r
        return
      end
      if ul <= l && r <= ur
        @tree[node] = val
        @lazy[node] = val
        return
      end
      push_down(node, l, r)
      mid = (l + r) / 2
      update_range(2 * node, l, mid, ul, ur, val)
      update_range(2 * node + 1, mid + 1, r, ul, ur, val)
      @tree[node] = @tree[2 * node] + @tree[2 * node + 1]
    end

    def query_range(node, l, r, ql, qr)
      if qr < l || ql > r
        return 0

      if ql <= l && r <= qr
        return @tree[node]

      push_down(node, l, r)
      mid = (l + r) / 2
      left = query_range(2 * node, l, mid, ql, qr)
      right = query_range(2 * node + 1, mid + 1, r, ql, qr)
      left + right
    end

    def push_down(node, l, r)
      if @lazy[node] != 0 && l != r
        mid = (l + r) / 2
        left = 2 * node
        right = 2 * node + 1
        @tree[left] = @lazy[node]
        @lazy[left] = @lazy[node]
        @tree[right] = @lazy[node]
        @lazy[right] = @lazy[node]
        @lazy[node] = 0
      end
    end
  end

        @tree[right] = @lazy[node]
